questions.question: Which office did Cyril Lucaris hold that the video describes as the closest analogue in Eastern Orthodoxy to a universal papal-style role (though not identical)?
What are the two stated goals of the video?
options: Debunking Calvinism and defending the Synod of Jerusalem

questions.question: Regarding scholarly views on whether Lucaris wrote his confession, what does Ortlund report?
options: There is no scholarly consensus either way
Only Protestant scholars accept Lucaris's authorship
Orthodox scholars unanimously reject the confession as a forgery
There is consensus outside Eastern Orthodoxy, and even many Orthodox scholars concede he wrote it
questions.answer: There is consensus outside Eastern Orthodoxy, and even many Orthodox scholars concede he wrote it

questions.question: Lucaris's pattern of being deposed, exiled, and reinstated is compared to Athanasius and said to have occurred roughly how many times?
Meletios Pegalios, a friend and supporter of Lucaris, was Archbishop of which see?
In what year was Lucaris's confession first published (as stated in the video)?
How many points or chapters of doctrine does the confession contain in the overview given?
In Lucaris's confession on Scripture, how does he relate the authority of Holy Scripture to that of the church?
options: He denies that the church has any authority
He teaches that church and Scripture are equally infallible
He subordinates Scripture to ecumenical councils
He ranks Scripture above the church on the basis of infallibility versus fallibility
questions.answer: He ranks Scripture above the church on the basis of infallibility versus fallibility

questions.question: The Confession of Dositheus is presented as teaching what regarding Scripture and the church?
options: Scripture alone is infallible; the church is fallible
The church is not inferior to Scripture; both are treated as infallible in the relevant sense
Only the Septuagint is authoritative for doctrine
Icons are the supreme rule of faith
questions.answer: The church is not inferior to Scripture; both are treated as infallible in the relevant sense

questions.question: On justification, how does Lucaris treat good works alongside sola fide (as summarized)?
options: Good works are necessary means to testify to faith and confirm calling, but justification is not grounded on them for judgment
Good works are unnecessary for Christians after justification
Good works are the formal cause of justification
Good works merit salvation independently of faith
questions.answer: Good works are necessary means to testify to faith and confirm calling, but justification is not grounded on them for judgment

questions.question: How many sacraments does Lucaris affirm?
What is Lucaris's position on transubstantiation and the Eucharist (as described)?
options: He reduces the Supper to mere memorial with no presence
He requires adoration of the host reserved on the altar
He rejects transubstantiation and speaks of real presence in a spiritual manner received by faith
questions.answer: He rejects transubstantiation and speaks of real presence in a spiritual manner received by faith

questions.question: Who does Lucaris identify as the sole head of the church in the confession (as cited)?
options: The college of apostles' successors collectively

questions.question: The wheat and tares language in Lucaris is used to illustrate what distinction?
options: Between the invisible church (elect) and hypocrites who may appear in visible churches
questions.answer: Between the invisible church (elect) and hypocrites who may appear in visible churches

questions.question: What does Lucaris say about purgatory in the confession's final chapter (as summarized)?
options: He commends prayers for the dead to shorten purgatory
He leaves purgatory as an open question
He calls purgatory a fiction; souls are in blessedness or damnation
He defines purgatory as identical to hell
questions.answer: He calls purgatory a fiction; souls are in blessedness or damnation

questions.question: Which synod does Philip Schaff compare—in its defining, systematizing role for Eastern Orthodoxy—to the Council of Trent for Roman Catholicism?
In what year was Lucaris canonized as a saint in the Eastern Orthodox Church (as stated)?
Which scholar does Ortlund cite regarding the century-long Protestant–Greek 'backstage parlay'?
In a letter quoted on the Eucharist, Lucaris affirms 'spiritual eating' such that the faithful do what?
options: Receive only a bare symbol with no benefit
Must perform works of supererogation before communion
Deny the reality of Christ's body and blood
Partake inwardly of Christ's true body and blood by faith, not only the outward sacrament
questions.answer: Partake inwardly of Christ's true body and blood by faith, not only the outward sacrament

questions.question: When a French ambassador questioned his confession, Lucaris famously refused to follow whom 'in the affairs of my belief and eternal salvation'?
Which New Testament-related project tied to Lucaris does the video mention for the year 1638?
options: A translation of the New Testament into Greek
The discovery of the Dead Sea Scrolls
questions.answer: A translation of the New Testament into Greek

questions.question: Toward the end of his life, Lucaris explicitly says he approves and adopts the doctrine of whom 'and all who agree with him'?
Lucaris links his confession to which Reformed confession as helping define the word 'Evangelical' (as displayed on screen)?
What role does Ortlund suggest Lucaris indirectly played regarding modern Eastern Orthodoxy's relationship to Protestantism?
options: He unified Orthodoxy and Protestantism permanently
He caused the East to adopt Reformed polity
He is largely irrelevant to later developments
The anti-Protestant Synod of Jerusalem was partly a response to him, helping define modern Orthodoxy against Protestantism
questions.answer: The anti-Protestant Synod of Jerusalem was partly a response to him, helping define modern Orthodoxy against Protestantism

questions.question: The YouTube video ID in the source URL is which string?
